analysis.overall_assessment: The single detected tea leaf shows clear signs of disease or environmental stress, with a prominent necrotic lesion indicating compromised quality. No healthy portions are visible.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Install windbreaks (e.g., bamboo screens or fast-growing shrubs) to reduce ozone exposure in vulnerable field sections
Apply foliar sprays of ascorbic acid (500 ppm) as an antioxidant shield during high-ozone periods
Shift harvesting to early morning when ozone concentrations are typically lowest

immediate_actions: Isolate and discard all leaves showing necrotic symptoms to prevent contamination of healthy batches
Conduct field-wide inspection within 48 hours to assess spread
Test soil pH and nutrient levels to rule out secondary stress factors
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Reject any leaf with visible necrotic spots; implement optical sorting with 0.5mm resolution for commercial batches
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Not suitable for orthodox or CTC processing; consider as waste for composting rather than processing
quality_preservation: Store healthy leaves in ventilated containers with humidity control (65-70%)
Process healthy leaves within 6 hours of harvest to minimize oxidation

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: None - the entire leaf is compromised by the necrotic lesion
alternative_uses: Use as green manure in non-tea crop fields after shredding
Ferment with rice bran for microbial inoculant production
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Chop leaves into 2-3cm pieces, layer with 3:1 carbon-rich material (e.g., dry grass), maintain 55-60°C for 14 days with weekly turning
prevention_measures: Install ozone monitoring stations at field perimeter
Plant ozone-tolerant cover crops like clover between tea rows
Implement precision irrigation to reduce plant stress

cost_saving_opportunities: Redirect labor to inspect neighboring bushes before symptoms spread
Use rejected leaves for on-farm compost to reduce fertilizer costs
value_recovery_methods: Convert waste into biochar for soil amendment (yields 25-30% carbon-rich product)

priority_actions: Immediately cordon off affected section and destroy symptomatic leaves
Install 2m high windbreaks within 72 hours to protect adjacent plants
Conduct air quality testing for ozone levels during peak sunlight hours
recommendations.timeline: Critical actions within 72 hours; full mitigation plan implementation within 30 days
monitoring_points: Daily visual inspection of new growth in adjacent plants
Soil nutrient analysis every 15 days for 2 months
